methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Andromeda Surgical is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Nick Damiano and Kartik Tiwari that has raised $10M+. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 11 private and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector, including peers such as Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics at the private end, and RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L among public benchmarks.

A single applicable method—stage-progression analysis—supports the valuation. Using total capital raised of $10M as a proxy for seed-stage value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier to the next funding round yields an implied valuation of $25M. This accounts for the typical acceleration in company value upon progression from seed to series A in the biotech space.

Peer-median triangulation could not be applied due to insufficient narrow-set comparables: while 16 peers exist in Biotech & Health broadly, none of the same-stage or adjacent-stage filters produced the three or more datapoints necessary for reliable medians. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median 6.6x across three tickers) is not feasible absent disclosed revenue for the target. The analysis therefore supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, centered on $25M, with material uncertainty driven by the reliance on a single method and the lack of direct peer financial benchmarks specific to seed-stage surgical biotech.
